<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# I need to figure out the shift cycle for positions 1 (col 22) and 2 (col 29)
# Current cursor is at col 15, need to move to col 22

# First move to col 22
action(['RIGHT'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
white_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'W']
cursor_col = min(p[1] for p in white_nodes[0]['boundary'])
print(f"Cursor at: {cursor_col}")

# Get initial hash for col 22
initial_22 = None
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'B' and n['pixels'] >= 10:
        b = n['boundary']
        min_r = min(p[0] for p in b)
        min_c = min(p[1] for p in b)
        if 50 <= min_r <= 57 and min_c == 22:
            initial_22 = n['hash']
            break

print(f"Initial col 22: {initial_22}")

# Press UP 4 times to see the cycle
cycle_22 = [initial_22]
for i in range(4):
    action(['UP'])
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        if n['color'] == 'B' and n['pixels'] >= 10:
            b = n['boundary']
            min_r = min(p[0] for p in b)
            min_c = min(p[1] for p in b)
            if 50 <= min_r <= 57 and min_c == 22:
                cycle_22.append(n['hash'])
                print(f"  {i+1} UP: {n['hash']}")

# Reset
for i in range(4):
    action(['DOWN'])

# Now move to col 29 and do the same
action(['RIGHT'])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
white_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'W']

print(f"\nCursor at: {cursor_col}")

initial_29 = None
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'B' and n['pixels'] >= 10:
        b = n['boundary']
        min_r = min(p[0] for p in b)
        min_c = min(p[1] for p in b)
        if 50 <= min_r <= 57 and min_c == 29:
            initial_29 = n['hash']
            break

print(f"Initial col 29: {initial_29}")

cycle_29 = [initial_29]
for i in range(4):
    action(['UP'])
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        if n['color'] == 'B' and n['pixels'] >= 10:
            b = n['boundary']
            min_r = min(p[0] for p in b)
            min_c = min(p[1] for p in b)
            if 50 <= min_r <= 57 and min_c == 29:
                cycle_29.append(n['hash'])
                print(f"  {i+1} UP: {n['hash']}")

# Reset
for i in range(4):
    action(['DOWN'])

# Now compile all info
top_pink_hashes = {
    (4, 22): 'cddd7d2c6a6ceea0',
    (4, 45): 'd38ad0a046a963a0',
    (13, 22): 'fb80c9b281b063da',
    (13, 45): 'f4469c244897540c',
    (22, 22): '12ee96cc93cd3e0a',
    (22, 45): 'b4edb3faf810133b'
}

print(f"\nShift cycles:")
print(f"  col 15: {['a2774f59f95f9997', 'b4edb3faf810133b', 'cddd7d2c6a6ceea0', '6610c6e38ccbd08b', '8b72057039a2be38']}")
print(f"  col 22: {cycle_22}")
print(f"  col 29: {cycle_29}")
print(f"  col 36: ['8b72057039a2be38', 'f4469c244897540c', ...]")
print(f"  col 43: ['cddd7d2c6a6ceea0', ...]")

# Check which top pink hash matches each cycle
print(f"\nMatches for col 22:")
for i, h in enumerate(cycle_22):
    for (r, c), th in top_pink_hashes.items():
        if h == th:
            print(f"  {i} UPs: matches top ({r}, {c})")

print(f"\nMatches for col 29:")
for i, h in enumerate(cycle_29):
    for (r, c), th in top_pink_hashes.items():
